description: We are going to update how features are managed on the local file system. Currently features are found in the features/ folder as markdown files. When a feature is processed it's filename is changed to _inprogress and finally _complete. TODO: - Intsead of changing the name to _inprogress, use the folder features/inprogress/ - Instead of changing the name to _complete, use the folder features/complete/ - If a feature fails or requires additional information, use folder features/hold - If you have questions leave the question as ## Information Needed in the hold file and when the user is ready, they can move the file with the answer back to the features/ folder. - Assign each feature a unique ID using nanoid or another URL compatible short uuid format.   Add this to the files as they are prcoessed as Feature ID: <unique id>.  Add the same ID to git commits as well. - When a feature is complete, add the complete summary package .json as features/log/<unique id>.json - As you write to inprogress, add a ## Plan section with the complete plan in a standard markdown format.
planPoints: 1. Add nanoid dependency (or implement short URL-safe ID generator) for unique feature IDs — using `crypto.randomBytes` with base64url encoding (no new dependency needed)

2. Update `feature.ts` — changed `markInProgress` to move to `features/inprogress/`, added `markHold` for `features/hold/`, added `generateFeatureId()` using `crypto.randomBytes`, updated `parseFeature` to detect state from parent dir name, kept legacy suffix detection for backward compat

3. Update `KaiBot.ts` — ensureFeaturesDir creates inprogress/complete/hold/log subdirs, markHold on errors with `## Information Needed`, writeFeatureLog for `features/log/<id>.json`, feature ID assigned via generateFeatureId and prepended to file, passed to commit
4. Update `commit.ts` — `buildCommitMessage` and `promptAndCommit` now accept optional featureId, appended as `[featureId]` to commit messages

5. Update tests in `feature.test.ts` — adapted all tests to folder-based transitions, added `markHold` and `generateFeatureId` test suites, updated `parseFeature` tests for directory-based state detection
6. Update tests in `KaiBot.test.ts` — adapted to inprogress/ folder moves, hold/ on error, ensureFeaturesDir creates all subdirs
7. Run typecheck, lint, and tests to verify everything passes — all pass (190 tests, 12 skipped smoke tests unrelated to changes)
summary: The feature management system was refactored to use folder-based state transitions (features/inprogress/, features/complete/, features/hold/) instead of filename suffixes, improving organization and clarity. Each feature is now assigned a unique 8-character ID that is prepended to the feature file and included in git commits, with completion logs stored in features/log/<id>.json. Failed features are moved to a hold folder with an Information Needed section, allowing users to resolve issues and retry processing.

description: # Web UI Feature ## Goal Update the KaiBot node application to spawn a web server on port 8500 (default) and host 127.0.0.1 (default). This should be a fast web server for KaiBot management with no large web frameworks.  The AI should generate the HTML where possible and only use packages as needed.   All page should be server-side generated. ## Login There will no login page for now.  In the future there may be so leave a placeholder for login but redirect immediately to /main with the primary contents. ## Navigation Menu The left side should feature a navigation menu with access to options we need in this website.  The navigations will follow the existing KaiBot options.  Each navigation option should have a hot key and complete screen reader compatibility. ## Top Status The top of the web ui should feature a status which shows the KaiBot name, a section for Current Project: <folder> which shows us the activate folder, model which shows us the current model and provider which shows us the LLM provider. Currently Anthropic is the supported provider and there are no options.  In the future "P" will be the hot key to change provider.   M will be the hot key to select a model from the provider and function as the existing M key does in the Ink.js UI. The WebUI will also have Estimated Spend Today: <Cost> which will be a total of features processed today. ## Main Page While in "Dashbaord" mode, the default, we will copy the concept from the Ink.JS UI using Websock for fast realtime status update messages, bot thinking, and so on. ## Dockable Windows Use a dockable window concept.  I suggest the rcdock component https: //github.com/ticlo/rc-dock. This will allow the user to adjust the size of the window for plan, file operations, command, thinking, etc ## Folders Design a folder concept for the website such as ./web/static/html ./web/static/images ./web/static/css and ./web/vendor/ for vendor specific modules that are not directly served from npm installed modules.
planPoints: 1. Create folder structure: web/static/css, web/static/html, web/static/images, web/vendor — created all directories
2. Install `ws` package for WebSocket support — installed ws + @types/ws
3. Create `src/web/WebServer.ts` — HTTP server using Node built-in `http` module on port 8500, host 127.0.0.1
4. Create `src/web/routes.ts` — route handler with login redirect (/ → /main), static file serving, and page generation
5. Create `src/web/templates.ts` — server-side HTML generation with top status, left nav, rc-dock container, and CDN imports for React + rc-dock
6. Create `web/static/css/main.css` — dark theme styles for layout, nav, status bar, dockable panels with rc-dock overrides
7. Create `web/static/html/client.js` — client-side JS with WebSocket reconnect, rc-dock layout (4 panels), real-time DOM updates
8. Create `src/web/wsHandler.ts` — bridges uiStore change events to WebSocket clients, sends full state on connect
9. Create `src/web/spendTracker.ts` — reads .kaibot/features.json, sums totalCostUsd for today's records
10. Integrate WebServer into `kai_bot.ts` — starts on port 8500, syncs model changes, shuts down on SIGINT/SIGTERM/quit
11. Run typecheck, lint, and fix any issues — all pass cleanly; existing tests still green
summary: A lightweight Web UI was added to KaiBot that runs on port 8500 alongside the existing Ink terminal UI, with a server-side generated dashboard featuring a dark-themed layout, top status bar showing project information and estimated daily spend, and a keyboard-accessible left navigation menu. The interface includes four dockable panels powered by rc-dock for monitoring thinking processes, commands, file operations, and plans, with real-time state updates flowing through WebSocket connections to all connected clients. New modules handle HTTP routing, HTML template generation, WebSocket message handling, and spend tracking from the bot's activity log.

description: Update the WebUI - Remove Model from the left, we already have it on the top. - Create a reusable popup menu where each selectable item have a keyboard values from 1 to 9 and up and down arrows to scroll if the list is longer than 9.  It should be mouse compatible as well. This popup menu should be tied to the M key or clicking on the model at the top. It should function like the InkUI version. Get a list of available models and present that to the user communicate back to the web server / KaiBot  and update the webui and InkJS
planPoints: 1. Remove the "Model" nav item from the left sidebar — removed from templates.ts
2. Make the model display in the top bar clickable — added role=button, tabindex, aria attrs in templates.ts
3. Create the reusable popup menu component in client.js — PopupMenu with 1-9 keys, arrows, Enter/Escape, mouse hover/click
4. Wire up M key and model click to open the popup — openModelSelector() fetches /api/models, opens popup anchored to trigger
5. Add API endpoint for model list and model change WebSocket message handling — /api/models route + ws "select-model" handler
6. Add CSS styles for the popup menu overlay — dark theme popup with scroll indicators, active badge, key badges
7. Handle model selection: send to server via WebSocket, update uiStore and web UI — ws.send select-model, uiStore.selectModel() broadcasts
summary: The Web UI was updated to remove the duplicate "Model" entry from the left sidebar and made the model display in the top bar clickable. A reusable PopupMenu component was created supporting both keyboard input (number keys 1–9 for quick selection, arrow keys to navigate, Enter/Escape to confirm/close) and mouse interaction (click to select, hover to highlight). The model selector, triggered by pressing M or clicking the model in the top bar, fetches available models from a new `/api/models` endpoint and sends selections back to the server via WebSocket to update all connected clients.

description: The WebUI is supposed to have resizing panels in the middle of the dashboard that receive text updates from the server through the websocket. This was described in the feature features/complete/web_ui.md Missing 1. Claude SDK Thinking Output is missing 2. Claude SDK Commands List is missing 3. File Operations is missing 4. Current Feature Being Processed is missing 5. Current Run time for the feature is missing 6. Current Stage (Review, THinking, Executing plan) is missing 7. Current Plan is missing These sections should be a in console-like scrollable sections with resizable areas of the dashbaord.
planPoints: 1. Add `featureStartTime` to UIState/store and expose it in WebUIState so the client can compute a live runtime — added to store.ts, wsHandler.ts, KaiAgent.ts

2. Increase store history limits (thinking lines, commands, file ops) so the web UI can show full scrollable history — store.ts limits raised to 200/100/100; terminal UI App.tsx now slices display locally

3. Add a "Feature Status" panel to the rc-dock layout showing current feature name, stage badge, and live runtime timer — client.js: added renderFeatureStatusContent, formatElapsed, runtime timer, updated layout and loadTab
4. Update client.js panel renderers to auto-scroll to bottom on new content (console-like behavior) — PanelWrapper._update() now checks scroll position and auto-scrolls to bottom
5. Add CSS styles for the new Feature Status panel and console-like scrollable appearance — main.css: added .status-section, .status-label, .status-value, .runtime-value styles
6. Verify typecheck passes and the build succeeds — typecheck, build, and lint all pass
summary: The KaiBot Web UI dashboard was enhanced with a new Feature Status panel displaying the current feature name, processing stage, live runtime timer, active model, and bot status, while store history limits were increased from 6/5/4 to 200/100/100 to enable full scrollable histories in console-like panels. Auto-scroll behavior was added to all panels to keep content pinned to the bottom as it streams in, with featureStartTime now tracked and broadcast via WebSocket for client-side runtime calculation.

description: If a feature added the the features folder duplicates the filename in inprogress or complete append the unique ID to the filename like "dupename-<unique id>.md" to avoid overwriting historical feature files.
planPoints: 1. Add `deconflictPath` helper in `feature.ts` that appends featureId to filename when target already exists — added exported helper using `existsSync`; falls back to `name-<id>.md` on collision
2. Apply deconfliction in `markInProgress`, `markComplete`, and `markHold` — all three transition functions now use `deconflictPath`; returned Feature reflects updated `name` and `filePath`
3. Add tests for the duplicate-name collision behavior in `feature.test.ts` — added `deconflictPath` unit tests plus collision tests for all three transition functions (30 tests, all pass)
summary: A `deconflictPath` helper was added to prevent filename collisions when feature files transition between states; if the target path exists, the feature's unique ID is appended to the filename stem (e.g., `dupename-<id>.md`). This deconfliction is applied in `markInProgress`, `markComplete`, and `markHold`, each returning an updated `Feature` with the new name and path to support chaining of subsequent transitions. The implementation includes 7 new test cases in `feature.test.ts`.

description: Create a webui with realtime updates from ClaudeSDK on the Web Attention, this is the 3rd request for this feature and it is still not working. Review the request carefully. Review the existing Web Server code in KaiBot carefully as well as the existing method used to update InkJS. TODO When in the Dashboard view on the Website there should be a console-like output for Claude Code SDK. InkJS currently shows Thinking, Commands, File Operations, and Plan.  The Web UI does not show anything.  It is blank.
planPoints: 1. Fix PanelWrapper to subscribe to a global state-change event and call forceUpdate() so panels re-render when new WebSocket data arrives — added panelBus EventTarget and _onStateChange handler in client.js
2. Dispatch the state-change event from updateDOM() after state is updated — replaced broken dockLayout.updateTab() calls with panelBus.dispatchEvent() in client.js
3. Clean up PanelWrapper event subscription on unmount to prevent memory leaks — added componentWillUnmount to remove event listener in client.js
4. Verify the fix by running typecheck and lint — both pass clean
summary: Fixed the blank dashboard panels in the web UI by implementing real-time updates through an event-driven architecture. When new WebSocket data arrives from the Claude SDK, a global event bus dispatches state-change events that trigger all panel components to re-render with the latest content (Thinking, Commands, File Operations, Plan, and Feature Status). This solution replaced a broken rc-dock update mechanism that was preventing React components from displaying the streamed information.

description: When using the WebUI to change model, I can't click on the menu item.  I can only select it with the keyboard.  Fix to enable common web usage, mouse over, click, etc
planPoints: 1. Refactor `renderPopupItems()` to remove the per-item click/mouseenter listeners it re-attaches each render — removed per-item listener attachment from `renderPopupItems()` in `web/static/html/client.js`

2. Add delegated `click` and `mouseover` listeners to the menu element once inside `showPopupMenu()`, so clicks survive innerHTML re-renders — added delegated listeners to the stable `menu` element in `showPopupMenu()` in `web/static/html/client.js`
summary: The model selector popup in the WebUI was fixed to respond to mouse clicks by switching from per-item event listeners to event delegation. The original issue was that `renderPopupItems()` was replacing the entire menu's HTML on every mouseover, destroying the DOM elements mid-click. The fix removes the per-item listeners and instead attaches stable `click` and `mouseover` listeners to the menu container itself, which now survive the HTML re-renders and allow normal mouse interactions.

description: Apply the following colors and notes to the webui design # 2. Color System ## Backgrounds | Usage           | Hex     | |-----------------|---------| | Main Background | #0B0F1A | | Card Background | #121826 | | Hover Surface   | #1A2233 | | Borders         | #2A344A | ## Accent Colors | Purpose        | Hex     | |----------------|---------| | Primary (Blue) | #3B82F6 | | Cyan Accent    | #22D3EE | | Purple Accent  | #8B5CF6 | | Success        | #22C55E | | Warning        | #F59E0B | | Error          | #EF4444 | ## Text Colors | Usage     | Hex     | |-----------|---------| | Primary   | #FFFFFF | | Secondary | #9CA3AF | | Disabled  | #6B7280 |
planPoints: 1. Update base/background colors in main.css (#0d1117 → #0B0F1A, #161b22 → #121826, #21262d → #1A2233, #30363d → #2A344A) — replaced all 4 background/border colors
2. Update primary blue accent (#58a6ff → #3B82F6, #1f6feb → #3B82F6) — all blue accents unified to #3B82F6
3. Update success/green colors (#3fb950 → #22C55E, #238636 → #16A34A, #56d364 → #22C55E) — all green accents updated
4. Update warning/yellow colors (#d29922 → #F59E0B) — warning color updated
5. Update error/red colors (#f85149 → #EF4444) — error color updated
6. Update purple accent colors (#d2a8ff → #8B5CF6) — purple accent updated
7. Update cyan accent colors (#56d4dd → #22D3EE) — cyan accent updated
8. Update text colors (#c9d1d9 → #FFFFFF, #8b949e → #9CA3AF, #484f58 → #6B7280) — all 3 text tiers updated
9. Update derived/tinted background colors to match new palette — updated 8 tinted backgrounds (badge, stage, file-op type backgrounds)
10. Verify CSS is valid by running typecheck/build — `npm run build` succeeds, no color refs in other web files
summary: The webui's color system was completely updated in `web/static/css/main.css` with new background, accent, and text colors creating a cohesive visual palette. All background shades, accent colors (blue, cyan, purple, success, warning, error), and text tiers were replaced, along with derived tinted backgrounds used throughout the UI.

description: Settings for the Model, Provider (Anthropic) and any other settings we add should be saved to a .kaibot/settings.json file which can be loaded upon project start to reload previous settings.
planPoints: 1. Create `src/settings.ts` with `KaiBotSettings` type, `loadSettings`, and `saveSettings` functions — created src/settings.ts
2. Update `src/kai_bot.ts` to load settings on startup and use saved model as fallback (env var > settings > default) — loadSettings called before model is resolved
3. Save settings to disk when the model changes via the `model-changed` event in `src/kai_bot.ts` — saveSettings called inside model-changed handler
4. Add tests for `src/settings.ts` in `src/__tests__/settings.test.ts` — 11 tests covering load/save/round-trip, all passing
summary: KaiBot now persists model and provider settings to `.kaibot/settings.json`, allowing configurations to survive bot restarts. At startup, the application loads saved settings using a priority system where environment variables override the settings file, which overrides the default `claude-opus-4-6` model. When users change the model through the UI, the new selection is automatically saved to disk.

description: Update the menu on the left of the WebUI for a new menu item "Features" which appears just under Dashboard. Change the hot key for "New Feature" to "N" Add the key "F" for the Features list. Pressing "F" or selecting Features from the menu will hide the Dashbaord converation and open a list of features.   The features list is a dock with 2 parts, the top is Pending  features and the bottom is Complete features. Complete features is taken from the json files in features/log/ Pending is taken from the list of files in features/ and features/hold/
planPoints: 1. Add `/api/features` endpoint in `routes.ts` to serve pending and complete feature lists — added `getFeaturesList()` helper and `/api/features` route in `routes.ts`

2. Update `templates.ts`: add "Features" nav item after Dashboard, change "New Feature" hotkey kbd to "N", add hidden `#features-view` HTML — updated `templates.ts` nav and added features-view main element
3. Add CSS for the features view (two-panel dock) to `main.css` — added `#features-view`, `#features-panels`, feature list item styles to `main.css`

4. Update `client.js`: change F hotkey to open features view, add N hotkey for new feature, add view switching logic and features rendering — added `showDashboardView()`, `showFeaturesView()`, `loadFeaturesData()`, render helpers, and updated keyboard/click handlers in `client.js`
summary: A new "Features" menu item was added to the left navigation (between Dashboard and New Feature) with hotkey F, while the "New Feature" hotkey was changed from F to N. Pressing F opens a two-panel dock displaying pending features (from `features/` and `features/hold/`) in the top panel and complete features (from `features/log/`) in the bottom panel, each with status badges and metadata. The implementation includes a new `/api/features` endpoint in the backend, HTML and CSS for the features view panels and list items, and updated client-side keyboard handlers and view-switching logic.

description: When a new feature request comes in, use the Claude Agent SDK to create a short title of 20-80 letters that title the change.  Add the title to the WebUI and InkJS UI while processing. Add the title to the log json file as well.
planPoints: 1. Add a `generateTitle()` function in KaiAgent.ts that uses the SDK (cheap model) to create a 20-80 character title from feature file content — added in src/KaiAgent.ts using claude-haiku-4-5
2. Add a `title` field to the `FeatureRecord` interface in featureDb.ts — added `title: string` field
3. Call `generateTitle()` early in `handleFeatureFile()` in KaiBot.ts and update uiStore.featureName with the generated title — generates title before agent starts, updates UI
4. Call `generateTitle()` early in `handleLinearIssue()` in KaiBot.ts (supplement with AI title if Linear title is just an identifier) — added title generation, falls back to issue.title
5. Pass the generated title into the feature record and log JSON output — title field added to both file and Linear records, and to CompleteFeature in routes.ts
6. Verify Web UI and Ink UI already display featureName — both UIs auto-update via uiStore; also updated client.js renderCompleteFeatures to prefer title field
7. Add tests for the `generateTitle()` function — 7 unit tests in src/__tests__/generateTitle.test.ts, all passing
summary: The feature implements AI-powered title generation for incoming feature requests using the Claude Agent SDK. When a new feature arrives—whether from a file or Linear issue—the system automatically generates a concise 20-80 character title using Claude Haiku, which is then displayed in both the Web and Ink terminal UIs and persisted in the feature database. The implementation includes comprehensive unit tests and updates the Web UI to prioritize the AI-generated titles over raw descriptions.

description: Selecting New Feature (N) from the web ui should open a popup dialog window.  This window should ask for a feature title and description. The feature description should be a Markdown editor such as ACE or any other light weight markdown text editor. The popup should have a Save button which submits it as a new feature to the server. The server will then assign a unique ID and save it to the features folder by unique id.md file. The popup also has a Hold button.  If hold is pressed, the file is still saved to the server but placed in the features/hold/ folder instead.
planPoints: 1. Add POST /api/features endpoint in routes.ts to accept title+description, generate unique ID, and save to features/ or features/hold/ — added readBody helper, POST /api/features handler with generateFeatureId
2. Add New Feature dialog HTML to templates.ts with title input, markdown textarea with toolbar, Save and Hold buttons — added dialog overlay with title input, md toolbar, textarea, Save/Hold buttons
3. Add CSS styles for the new feature dialog in main.css — added dialog overlay, box, header, body, toolbar, textarea, buttons, error styles

4. Add client-side JS in client.js for opening/closing the dialog, markdown toolbar actions, and submitting via fetch POST — added openNewFeatureDialog, closeNewFeatureDialog, submitNewFeature, applyMarkdownAction
5. Wire up the N key shortcut and nav-feature click to open the new feature dialog — updated keydown handler, added click handlers for nav-feature, dialog buttons, overlay close, md toolbar
6. Verify typecheck and lint pass — both npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass cleanly
title: New Feature Dialog with Markdown Editor
summary: A New Feature dialog popup was implemented in the KaiBot web UI that opens when pressing N or clicking the New Feature nav item. The dialog includes a title field and a markdown editor with a formatting toolbar, along with Save and Hold buttons that submit the feature via a new `POST /api/features` endpoint. The endpoint generates a unique 8-character ID and saves the feature to either the `features/` or `features/hold/` folder depending on which button is clicked.
